examples.title: Basic BGP Configuration
examples.description: Shows basic BGP neighbor configuration for eBGP and iBGP setup
examples.code:
router bgp 100
 ! Configure eBGP neighbor
 neighbor 192.168.1.2 remote-as 200
 neighbor 192.168.1.2 description eBGP-to-AS200
 ! Configure iBGP neighbor
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 remote-as 100
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 description iBGP-peer
 ! Optional: Modify next-hop for iBGP
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 next-hop-self
examples.explanation: Basic BGP configuration establishes peering with neighbors. remote-as defines the AS number of the neighbor. next-hop-self changes the NEXT_HOP attribute for routes advertised to iBGP peers.

examples.title: BGP Route Summarization
examples.description: Demonstrates BGP route aggregation with and without AS_SET
examples.code:
! Without AS_SET (simple aggregation)
router bgp 100
 aggregate-address 172.16.0.0 255.255.0.0

! With AS_SET (preserves path information)
router bgp 100
 aggregate-address 172.16.0.0 255.255.0.0 as-set

! Summary-only (suppresses specific routes)
router bgp 100
 aggregate-address 172.16.0.0 255.255.0.0 summary-only
examples.explanation: aggregate-address creates a summary route. as-set option includes AS_PATH information from component routes, useful for loop prevention. summary-only suppresses advertising the more specific routes.

examples.title: BGP Path Attribute Configuration
examples.description: Shows how to manipulate BGP attributes for traffic engineering
examples.code:
! Set Local Preference for outbound traffic
router bgp 100
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 route-map SET_LOCAL_PREF in
route-map SET_LOCAL_PREF permit 10
 set local-preference 200

! Set MED for inbound traffic control
router bgp 100
 neighbor 192.168.1.2 route-map SET_MED out
route-map SET_MED permit 10
 set metric 50

! Filter routes using prefix-list
router bgp 100
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 prefix-list BLOCKED_ROUTES in
ip prefix-list BLOCKED_ROUTES deny 10.0.0.0/8
ip prefix-list BLOCKED_ROUTES permit 0.0.0.0/0 le 32
examples.explanation: Local Preference influences outbound traffic (higher wins). MED influences inbound traffic from neighboring AS (lower wins). Prefix lists filter which routes are accepted or advertised.

examples.title: BGP Route Reflection Configuration
examples.description: Shows configuration of route reflector and its clients
examples.code:
! Route Reflector Server
router bgp 100
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 remote-as 100
 neighbor 10.1.1.1 route-reflector-client
 neighbor 10.1.2.1 remote-as 100
 neighbor 10.1.2.1 route-reflector-client
 neighbor 10.1.3.1 remote-as 100
 ! 10.1.3.1 is non-client

! On Client Router
router bgp 100
 neighbor 10.0.0.1 remote-as 100
 ! Client only needs to peer with RR
examples.explanation: route-reflector-client defines which iBGP peers are clients. RR reflects routes from clients to all other RR peers (clients and non-clients). Non-clients require full mesh with other RR.

examples.title: BGP UPDATE Message Processing
examples.description: Algorithm for processing received BGP UPDATE messages
examples.code:
PROCESS_UPDATE(update_msg):
 1. FOR each withdrawn_route IN update_msg.withdrawn_routes:
 Remove route from BGP table
 Mark as withdrawn

 2. FOR each path_attribute IN update_msg.path_attributes:
 Parse and store attributes
 (AS_PATH, NEXT_HOP, LOCAL_PREF, MED, etc.)

 3. IF own_AS IN path_attribute.AS_PATH:
 DISCARD route (loop detected)
 RETURN

 4. Add own_AS to beginning of AS_PATH

 5. IF route already exists in BGP table:
 IF new_route better than existing (path selection):
 Replace existing route
 Advertise to all BGP peers
 ELSE:
 Keep existing route
 ELSE:
 Add new route to BGP table
 Advertise to all BGP peers

 6. UPDATE routing decision process
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how BGP processes incoming UPDATE messages, including loop detection using AS_PATH, path comparison, and route advertisement to peers.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of prefixes

examples.title: Route Flap Damping Configuration
examples.description: Shows configuration to suppress unstable BGP routes
examples.code:
router bgp 100
 ! Enable route flap damping
 bgp dampening

! Custom damping parameters
bgp dampening 15 750 2000 60
 ! half-life: 15 min
 ! reuse: 750
 ! suppress: 2000
 ! max-suppress-time: 60 min

! View dampened routes
show ip bgp dampened-paths

! Clear damping for specific route
clear ip bgp 10.0.0.0 dampening
examples.explanation: Route flap damping suppresses unstable routes. When a route flaps, penalties accumulate. Once the penalty exceeds suppress limit, route is dampened. Penalty decays over time based on half-life.
